analysis: Feasibility of soft routing vs IRIS binary natural-stop
finding: Soft routing over binary natural-stop provides no net accuracy gain on this workload. At tau=0.90, uncertain subset (n=70) already has 82.9% accuracy — stage-2 escalation (72% on escalated hard subset) would likely degrade it. Binary natural-stop threshold is empirically well-calibrated for IRIS.
future_work: Soft routing may help when stage-0 confidence signals include log-probabilities or learned question embeddings (not available in current runs). Collecting logprob data requires --log_logprobs flag on IRIS runs.
